Package neqsim.process.util.optimizer

Class ProcessModelSimulationEvaluator.ParameterDefinition

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.util.optimizer.ProcessModelSimulationEvaluator.ParameterDefinition
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
Enclosing class:
ProcessModelSimulationEvaluator
public static class ProcessModelSimulationEvaluator.ParameterDefinition extends Object implements Serializable
Definition of a process-model decision variable.
Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team

  • Constructor Details

    • ParameterDefinition

      public ParameterDefinition()
      Default constructor for serialization frameworks.

    • ParameterDefinition

      public ParameterDefinition(String address, double lowerBound, double upperBound, String unit)
      Creates a decision variable whose name is the same as its address.
      Parameters:
      address - the area-qualified automation address
      lowerBound - lower optimization bound
      upperBound - upper optimization bound
      unit - unit of measure used when setting the value

    • ParameterDefinition

      public ParameterDefinition(String name, String address, double lowerBound, double upperBound, String unit)
      Creates a decision variable.
      Parameters:
      name - human readable parameter name
      address - the area-qualified automation address
      lowerBound - lower optimization bound
      upperBound - upper optimization bound
      unit - unit of measure used when setting the value

      public BiConsumer<ProcessModel,Double> getSetter()
      Gets the optional custom setter.
      Returns:
      custom setter, or null when automation should be used

      public boolean isWithinBounds(double value)
      Checks whether a value is inside the declared bounds.
      Parameters:
      value - value to test
      Returns:
      true when the value is inside the declared bounds

    • clamp

      public double clamp(double value)
      Clamps a value to the declared bounds.
      Parameters:
      value - value to clamp
      Returns:
      value limited to the inclusive lower and upper bounds
